perturbation.original_text: 5. The Confidential Information shall remain at all times the property of the Company. No rights to use, license, or otherwise exploit the Confidential Information are granted to the Recipient, by implication or otherwise, by virtue of Confidential Information being made available to the Recipient or any of the Recipient’s Representatives .
perturbation.changed_text: 5. The Confidential Information shall remain at all times the property of the Company, unless a Definitive Agreement is reached. Upon execution of a Definitive Agreement, the rights to use, license, or otherwise exploit the Confidential Information shall be governed by the terms of that Definitive Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By adding the condition about a Definitive Agreement, Section 5 now contradicts Section 16. Section 16 states that neither party will be under any legal obligation until a Definitive Agreement is entered into, except with respect to the matters specifically agreed to in this Confidentiality Agreement. However, the modified Section 5 implies that the terms of the Confidentiality Agreement regarding the ownership and use of Confidential Information can be superseded by a Definitive Agreement, creating uncertainty about which agreement governs.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Recipient agrees that, unless and until a D efinitive Agreement is entered into between the Receiver and the Recipient with respect to the Tran saction, neither the Receiver nor the Recipient will be under any legal obligation of any kind whatsoever with respect to the Transaction by virtue of this or any other written or oral expression, except with respect to the matters specifically agreed to herein. Except for the matters set forth in this Agreement or in any Definitive Agreement, neither party shall be entitl ed to rely on any statement, promise, agreement or understanding, whether oral or written, any cust om, usage of trade, course of dealing or conduct.
perturbation.justification: The original text of Section 5 stated that the Confidential Information remains the property of the Company at all times, with no rights granted to the Recipient. The changed text adds a condition that if a Definitive Agreement is reached, the rights to use, license, or exploit the Confidential Information shall be governed by that Definitive Agreement. This directly contradicts Section 16, which states that neither party will be under any legal obligation until a Definitive Agreement is entered into, except with respect to matters specifically agreed to in the Confidentiality Agreement. The modified Section 5 introduces uncertainty about which agreement governs the use of Confidential Information, creating an in-text contradiction as it implies that the Definitive Agreement can supersede the terms of the Confidentiality Agreement regarding the Confidential Information, while Section 16 states the Confidentiality Agreement is what remains unless otherwise specified.

perturbation.original_text: 9. The Receiver may elect at any time to terminate further access by the Recipient to the Confidential Information. If at any time the Recip ient determines not to proceed with the possible Transaction, the Recipient will promptly notify the Receiver in writing. Following any request by the Receiver or any of its Representatives, the Rec ipient agrees (i) to promptly re-deliver to the Receiver all written Confidential Information and a ny other written material containing or reflecting any of the Confidential Information in the possessi on of the Recipient or the Recipient's Representatives, (ii) the Recipient and the Recipie nt's Representatives will not retain any copies, extracts or other reproductions in whole or in part , mechanical or electronic, of such written material, and (iii) all Notes prepared by the Recip ient or the Recipient's Representatives will be destroyed, with all such destruction being confirme d by the Recipient to the Receiver in writing.
perturbation.changed_text: 9. The Receiver may elect at any time to terminate further access by the Recipient to the Confidential Information. If at any time the Recip ient determines not to proceed with the possible Transaction, the Recipient will promptly notify the Receiver in writing. Following any request by the Receiver or any of its Representatives, the Rec ipient agrees (i) to promptly re-deliver to the Receiver all written Confidential Information and a ny other written material containing or reflecting any of the Confidential Information in the possessi on of the Recipient or the Recipient's Representatives, (ii) the Recipient and the Recipie nt's Representatives will not retain any copies, extracts or other reproductions in whole or in part , mechanical or electronic, of such written material, and (iii) all Notes prepared by the Recip ient or the Recipient's Representatives will be archived securely but not destroyed, with the confirmation of such archiving provided to the Receiver in writing.
perturbation.explanation: The change in Section 9, which replaces the requirement to destroy 'Notes' with a requirement to 'archive securely,' creates a direct conflict with Section 11. Section 11 specifically addresses computer files created during backups and mandates non-use and confidentiality 'in perpetuity.' The amendment in Section 9 now suggests that 'Notes,' which could also exist in digital form, are not subject to the same 'in perpetuity' obligation, creating uncertainty about the required handling and duration of confidentiality for different types of Confidential Information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Notwithstanding Section 9 or anything to the co ntrary in this Agreement, the Recipient and its Representatives are not required to destroy any com puter files containing the Confidential Information that are created during automatic compu ter system backup, provided that such files are stored securely by the Recipient and its Repres entatives, cannot be destroyed without undue efforts, and access to such files are limited. With respect to such backup computer files, the non- use and confidentiality obligations set forth in th is Agreement shall apply in perpetuity and survive expiration or termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The change in Section 9 modifies the requirement for 'Notes' from 'destroyed' to 'archived securely'. Section 11 states that computer files created during backups are subject to non-use and confidentiality obligations 'in perpetuity'. The modified Section 9 creates a contradiction because 'Notes' which could exist in digital form, are not explicitly subject to the same 'in perpetuity' obligation as backup files, creating uncertainty and inconsistency regarding the handling and duration of confidentiality for different types of Confidential Information and therefore a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 11. Notwithstanding Section 9 or anything to the co ntrary in this Agreement, the Recipient and its Representatives are not required to destroy any com puter files containing the Confidential Information that are created during automatic compu ter system backup, provided that such files are stored securely by the Recipient and its Repres entatives, cannot be destroyed without undue efforts, and access to such files are limited. With respect to such backup computer files, the non- use and confidentiality obligations set forth in th is Agreement shall apply in perpetuity and survive expiration or termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 11. Notwithstanding Section 9 or anything to the co ntrary in this Agreement, the Recipient and its Representatives are not required to destroy any com puter files containing the Confidential Information that are created during automatic compu ter system backup, provided that such files are stored securely by the Recipient and its Repres entatives, and access to such files are limited. With respect to such backup computer files, the non- use and confidentiality obligations set forth in th is Agreement shall apply for a period of five (5) years and survive expiration or termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction. The original section states that confidentiality obligations for backup computer files apply 'in perpetuity'. The modified section limits this to a period of 'five (5) years.' This creates an in-text contradiction regarding the duration of the confidentiality obligations for this specific type of Confidential Information. If the agreement is terminated after 3 years, is the backup information still confidential?
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement will continue indefinitely, unle ss otherwise agreed in writing by the Receiver.
perturbation.justification: The original text states the confidentiality obligations for backup computer files apply 'in perpetuity'. The changed text modifies this to a period of 'five (5) years'. The contradicted text states the agreement will continue indefinitely. Therefore, the changed text introduces a contradiction regarding the duration of the confidentiality obligations for backup files, creating a strong in-text contradiction because it changes a perpetual duration to a limited duration, when the agreement is meant to last indefinitely.

perturbation.original_text: 22. This Agreement will continue indefinitely, unle ss otherwise agreed in writing by the Receiver.
perturbation.changed_text: 22. This Agreement will continue indefinitely, unless terminated by either party with thirty (30) days written notice.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction. The original clause states the agreement continues indefinitely unless otherwise agreed in writing by the Receiver. The change allows *either* party to terminate with 30 days notice, which directly contradicts the requirement of the Receiver's agreement for termination. It makes the agreement open ended or not, based on the whim of a party.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the parties hereto and supersedes all prior contracts, agreements and understandings pert aining to the subject matter of this Agreement. No modification or alteration of this A greement shall be binding unless executed in writing by the parties hereto. There are no repres entations, warranties, collateral agreements or conditions affecting this transaction other than as are expressed or referred to herein in writing.
perturbation.justification: The original text states the agreement continues indefinitely unless otherwise agreed in writing by the Receiver. The changed text allows either party to terminate with 30 days notice, directly contradicting the requirement of the Receiver's agreement for termination. This creates an inconsistency as the agreement is both indefinite (as per the original text) and terminable by either party (as per the changed text), leading to an in-text contradiction as defined by 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'. Furthermore, the contradicted text states that no modification or alteration of the agreement shall be binding unless executed in writing by the parties hereto. The changed text alters the original agreement without the explicit agreement of both parties, introducing conflict.
